prose:
Fukui reactivity analysis of phenol was computed using DFT with B3LYP functional and def2-TZVP basis set (tier standard, density-fit via PySCF) in gas phase, charge 0, multiplicity 1. The calculation derived per-atom Fukui indices (f⁺, f⁻) and dual descriptor by evaluating Mulliken partial charges on the neutral molecule and on separately-computed cation (+1, doublet) and anion (−1, doublet) states.

The most electrophilic site is atom 3 (ortho-carbon, f⁺ = 2.103), indicating strong susceptibility to nucleophilic attack. The most nucleophilic site is the oxygen atom (atom 1, f⁻ = 0.162), consistent with the hydroxyl group's lone-pair donor character. The dual descriptor confirms that the aromatic carbons ortho and para to the oxygen (atoms 3, 4, 7) are strongly electrophilic, while the oxygen is nucleophilic.

SCF converged for all three molecular states. Fukui charge conservation passed for both f⁺ and f⁻ (sum = 1.0000). Geometry was taken from the user-supplied .xyz without re-optimization at the DFT level. No warnings reported. Integrity status: ok (trustworthy).</prose>
<parameter name="n_atoms">13
